evals.prompt: There are markdown docs under docs/ that lack frontmatter. Run bootstrap_frontmatter.py --write to stamp them, then run check_design_doc_freshness.py to detect a stale doc (one whose cited source file changed since source_commit).
evals.expected_output: bootstrap_frontmatter stamps all docs with type/topic/source_commit/manual_edit; check_design_doc_freshness.py then prints 'STALE <doc>' for the doc whose cited source changed and exits 1.

assertions.text: After bootstrap --write, every .md in docs/ has all 4 required frontmatter keys (type, topic, source_commit, manual_edit)
assertions.evidence: Isolated git repo: after --write, every docs/*.md parsed with all of {type, topic, source_commit, manual_edit}.

assertions.text: source_commit values look like a 40-char hex sha
check_design_doc_freshness.py exits 1 and prints STALE for the doc whose cited source file was modified after its source_commit
assertions.evidence: Modified app/publisher.py after commit -> 'STALE docs/overview.md (... 1 cited source file(s) changed: app/publisher.py)', exit 1.

assertions.text: Docs that already have all 4 keys are not modified by bootstrap (idempotent)
assertions.evidence: Pre-stamped already.md reported as '1 already complete' and left unmodified by --write.

evals.prompt: Given an existing doc with both <!-- @generated --> and <!-- @user --> regions, run sentinel_merge.py with a new generated doc. The @user block must be preserved byte-identical; the @generated region must contain the new content.
evals.expected_output: The merged output contains the new @generated content and the original @user block unchanged.

assertions.text: Merged output contains the new generated content (not the old)
assertions.evidence: sentinel_merge.py --demo: all assertions passed (generated region carries new content).

assertions.text: The @user block text is byte-identical to the original
assertions.evidence: sentinel_merge.py --demo asserts the @user block is preserved byte-identical.

assertions.text: sentinel_merge demo() passes all its assert statements without error
When existing doc has no sentinels, full overwrite is applied and a warning is printed to stderr
assertions.evidence: stderr: 'sentinel_merge: existing doc has no sentinels — full overwrite applied'.

evals.prompt: Given docs/overview.md that mentions `app/publisher.py` in its body, and a changed file list of ['app/publisher.py'], affected_docs.py should include docs/overview.md in its output.
evals.expected_output: docs/overview.md is printed to stdout; docs/unrelated.md (which does not cite publisher.py) is not printed.

assertions.text: docs/overview.md appears in the output when app/publisher.py is the changed file
assertions.evidence: affected_docs.py --demo: all assertions passed (citing doc selected).

assertions.text: docs/unrelated.md does NOT appear in the output
assertions.evidence: affected_docs.py --demo asserts the non-citing doc is excluded.

assertions.text: affected_docs demo() passes all its assert statements without error
When graphify is unavailable, falls back to basename/stem matching without error
assertions.evidence: --demo exercises the graphify-absent path via basename/stem matching with no error.
